_comment: WinTProxy traffic-stage configuration. Top-level sections mirror the runtime flow: capture -> dns -> bypass -> policy -> proxy -> logging.

capture._comment: WinDivert adapter settings. The filter captures TCP/UDP packet paths and relay/DNS returns; queue knobs tune the driver queue.

dns._comment: DNS is a first-class stage before bypass/policy. Matching TCP/UDP port 53 traffic is redirected when enabled.

bypass._comment: Non-proxyable/direct destinations. DNS queries are handled before these checks; non-DNS traffic hits bypass before policy.

policy._comment: Ordered first-match rules. Decisions are proxy/direct only; block is intentionally removed from the traffic model.

rules._comment: Let the local SOCKS5 server talk directly so we don't proxy-loop ourselves.
Direct-connect private/RFC 1918 ranges. You can alternatively set bypass.private_ips=true.
Proxy these specific browsers. Process matching supports wildcards: prefix* *suffix *contains*.
Proxy common web ports for all other processes. Port ranges use dash notation.
Catch-all. First-match-wins ordering means earlier direct rules take priority.

proxy._comment: SOCKS5 proxy server address and port. Must be an IPv4 literal; authentication is not supported.

logging._comment: level is one of: error, warn, info, debug, trace. info is lifecycle/config only; debug adds flow decisions, DNS query summaries, and grouped performance snapshots; trace adds packet/protocol internals. file is optional; empty means stderr only.
